ip_outcome_note: No IP flag. Item card functioning as a 70-HP Colorless Pokemon proxy for fossil evolution lines.

trivia_bullets.text: Rare Fossil was released in Darkness Ablaze (August 14, 2020), the third Sword & Shield series expansion. It was originally released in the Japanese Infinity Zone expansion (June 2020) and reprinted in Japanese Shiny Star V. [Bulbapedia: Rare Fossil (Darkness Ablaze 167), via WebSearch; TCGCodex: DAA 167, via WebSearch]

Rare Fossil plays as an Item card but functions as a 70-HP Basic Colorless Pokemon on the bench — a mechanic descending from the original Fossil card mechanic introduced in the Base Set era. It enables players to bench fossil-type Pokemon as placeholders before evolving them. At 70 HP, it holds the highest HP total among all Fossil Item cards printed to the Darkness Ablaze era. [Pojo.com: Rare Fossil review, via WebSearch; Bulbapedia: Rare Fossil DAA 167, via WebSearch]

Illustrated by 5ban Graphics. The stone specimen displays multiple embedded fossil impressions — a crab or trilobite-like arthropod, a spiral shell, and a fish-like form — representing the range of fossil Pokemon the card can be used to support. The display-specimen framing (lit from above, displayed against a soft gradient) mirrors museum natural history presentation. [Frontmatter artist field; visual read from Vision section]
